data.job_title: Coordinador(a) de Desarrollo, Perforación y Tronadura | Spence
data.description:
**Sobre Spence | BHP**
Spence es una operación minera ubicada en la Región de Antofagasta, reconocida por su liderazgo en innovación y sostenibilidad. Como parte de una empresa global dedicada a los recursos naturales, con presencia en más de 90 ubicaciones alrededor del mundo, impulsamos un modelo de minería autónoma que integra tecnología avanzada, eficiencia y seguridad, marcando el camino hacia el futuro.
  
  
Nuestro compromiso es generar valor de manera responsable, cuidando el medioambiente y poniendo a las personas en el centro de todo lo que hacemos.
  
  
**¿Por qué Spence | BHP?**
En Spence creemos que el talento es la fuerza que transforma la minería. Aquí encontrarás oportunidades para crecer en un entorno inclusivo, diverso y orientado al aprendizaje continuo. Ser parte de Spence significa contribuir a proyectos que hacen historia, trabajando con equipos que comparten un propósito claro: innovar para construir un mundo más seguro, sostenible y humano. Tu voz cuenta, tu desarrollo importa y tu trabajo genera impacto real.
  
  
**El desafío que te espera**
En Spence estamos buscando a quien asuma el rol de Coordinador(a) de Mina, con la misión de generar la coordinación, planificación y seguimiento integral de la ejecución operativa y de los procesos unitarios de los planes mineros, asegurando su cumplimiento. Lo harás en conformidad con los objetivos de HSE, costos, volumen, calidad, estándares operacionales, procedimientos, instrucciones de trabajo y legislación vigente.
  
  
Serás responsable de realizar el seguimiento del desempeño de producción mina, además de desarrollar y proponer innovación tecnológica para el área.
  
  
**Principales Responsabilidades**

- Demostrar un compromiso permanente con la seguridad de las personas y el cero daño, priorizando este valor por sobre la producción.
- Coordinar y gestionar con distintas áreas y stakeholders aspectos técnicos, económicos y de resultados, con foco en el cumplimiento sostenible del plan minero y de los procesos unitarios en mina a cielo abierto.
- Realizar control operativo y seguimiento de los KPI de operaciones mina, incluyendo movimiento mina, extracción, alimentación a chancado, rendimientos y utilización de equipos.
- Generar planes de producción, habilitadores y tácticos, integrando inputs de la línea operativa junto a planificación, mantenimiento mina y control de procesos.
- Desarrollar, coordinar y controlar habilitadores y la ejecución del plan minero.
- Reportar directamente al Superintendente de Desarrollo, Perforación y Tronadura todos los aspectos de la operación minera.
- Reportar e investigar eventos que estén por debajo del umbral de riesgo definido por el negocio.
- Aplicar y liderar el modelo operativo BOS, impulsando la mejora continua.
- Promover un ambiente laboral inclusivo, diverso y de respeto, orientado al alto desempeño.

**Lo que esperamos de ti**

- Formación en Ingeniería Civil Mina.
- Experiencia demostrable en procesos productivos mineros a cielo abierto (carguío, transporte, perforación, tronadura, desarrollo mina y caminos, planificación y/o análisis y mejoramiento), con 8 años de experiencia total y al menos 5 años específicos en D&B.
- Capacidad de liderazgo y trabajo en equipo.
- Deseable postítulo en tronadura.
- Deseable nivel de inglés intermedio.
- Manejo de software Vulcan.
- Licencia de conducir clase B.
- Licencia de Programador(a) Calculista de Explosivos (excluyente).
- Disponibilidad para trabajar en modalidad de turno 7x7 diurno en faena Spence.

**Por qué te encantará trabajar aquí**
En Spence | BHP entendemos que para dar lo mejor, necesitas sentirte bien. Por eso, nuestros beneficios están diseñados para acompañarte en cada etapa, cuidando tu salud, impulsando tu desarrollo y promoviendo un equilibrio real entre tu vida personal y laboral.
  
  

- Desarrollo profesional: Formación continua y oportunidades reales de crecimiento.
- Remuneración: justa y competitiva que refleje tu rol, habilidades y logros.
- Cultura inclusiva: Promovemos un entorno laboral donde cada persona se siente valorada, respetada y libre de expresar su identidad auténtica.
- Reconocimiento: Te recompensamos por tus pequeños y grandes logros a través de nuestro programa global de reconocimiento Big Thanks | Muchas Gracias.

¡Y mucho más!
  
  
**Sobre nuestro proceso**
En Spence | BHP contratamos personas que se alinean con nuestros valores y cumplen con los requisitos del rol. Como parte del proceso, podríamos someter a revisión el cumplimiento efectivo de los requisitos informados para determinar la idoneidad y capacidad personal para ejecutar el trabajo.
  
  
*Si ya trabajas en BHP, postula a través del portal interno con tu correo corporativo.*
**¿Y por qué no tú?**
Con operaciones en todo el mundo, nuestra compañía te ofrece oportunidades sin límites. Desde beneficios competitivos hasta la posibilidad de trabajar en distintos países, aquí puedes diseñar tu carrera a tu manera. Si estás buscando un lugar donde tu trabajo tenga propósito y tu crecimiento sea parte del camino, este puede ser tu próximo paso.
  
  
¡Postula hoy y sé parte de la transformación de la minería!
  
  
**Apoyamos una fuerza laboral diversa**
En Spence | BHP nos adherimos a la Ley de Inclusión 21.015.
  
  
Sabemos que la diversidad nos fortalece. Es por eso por lo que estamos enfocados en crear una fuerza laboral más diversa que represente las comunidades en las que operamos y vivimos, asegurando un ambiente laboral donde cada persona sea incluida y tratada con respeto.
  
  
En ese sentido, estamos comprometidos a realizar un proceso de selección justo, equitativo y accesible para todos. Si tienes algún tipo de discapacidad, por favor escríbenos a inclusion@bhp.com para poder ajustar nuestro proceso de selección y brindarte el apoyo necesario.

company_object.name: BHP
company_object.long_description:
We are supplying the resources the world needs to help build a better, clearer future. Copper for renewable energy. Potash for sustainable farming. Iron ore and metallurgical coal for the steel needed for global infrastructure and the energy transition. #FutureIsClear

Across our global operations, we are committed to working in ways that are true to our BHP Charter values of Sustainability, Integrity, Respect, Performance, Simplicity and Accountability. Learn more about working at BHP and the exciting career opportunities that exist for professionals, undergraduates and graduates on our website: www.bhp.com.

data.job_title: Integrated Planner - Training & Performance | FFA | Operations Services
data.description:
**About BHP**
At BHP we support our people to grow, learn, develop their skills and reach their potential. With a global portfolio of operations, we offer a diverse and inclusive environment with extraordinary career opportunities. Our strategy is to focus on creating a safe work environment where our employees feel strongly connected to our values and objectives, and where the capability of our people is key to our success.
  
  
Come and be a part of this success.
  
  
**About BHP Operations Services**
BHP Operations Services delivers industry leading mining services across BHP operations Australia-wide with specialist capabilities in load and haul, fixed plant maintenance and mobile maintenance.
  
  
BHP Operations Services has a strong focus on people capability and culture. Our people are our greatest asset and are at the centre of everything that we do. We focus on high performance safety and productivity through our people. With world-class training that is unmatched in the industry and the opportunity to advance your career across locations and commodities, join the team and make your place in BHP.
  
  
We are investing in our team to take your skills and career to places you never thought possible. This is an exciting time for BHP. Are you ready to make your place?
  
  
**About The Role**
We are seeking a high-performing Integrated Planner – Training & Performance to support the BHP FutureFit Academy (FFA). This role is responsible for planning, scheduling and performance analysis to enable safe, reliable training delivery across sites in QLD and WA.
  
  
You will develop integrated training schedules, optimise trainer utilisation and deliver data-driven insights to support decision-making and continuous improvement. This is a key role connecting training delivery, workforce planning and performance reporting to ensure FFA operates as a high-performing system.
  
  
Key Responsibilities:Lead integrated planning and scheduling across FFA, translating demand into executable plans and optimising training delivery across QLD and WA.
  
  

- Manage short to long-term schedules, resolve conflicts and risks, and ensure delivery, compliance and LMS performance targets are met
- Optimise trainer utilisation by balancing capacity vs demand and adjusting workforce allocation
- Develop Power BI dashboards and reporting to track utilisation, performance and delivery outcomes
- Analyse data to identify trends, gaps and improvement opportunities, providing clear insights to support decision-making
- Embed BOS planning routines, improve planning accuracy and drive continuous improvement initiatives
- Partner across Superintendents, Trainers and support teams to align planning, acting as the central coordination point for delivery execution.

**About You**

- Proven experience in planning and scheduling within operational or training environments
- Advanced capability using Microsoft Project to build and manage integrated schedules
- Strong experience with SAP (1SAP) and working across enterprise systems
- Demonstrated ability to balance capacity vs demand and optimise workforce utilisation
- Proven capability developing Power BI dashboards and performance reporting
- Strong analytical skills with ability to translate data into actionable insights
- Highly organised and detail-focused, able to manage competing priorities.

**About Our Process**
At BHP, we are committed to employing individuals who align with the BHP Charter Values and meet the requirements of the role. As part of the recruitment process, there are a number of checks which may be conducted to demonstrate applicants suitability for a role including police / criminal background checks, medical, drug and alcohol testing, due diligence checks, right to work checks, and/or reference checks.
  
  
**Supporting a Diverse Workforce**
The size, stability and magnitude of our business not only provides significant opportunity for professional development, but also attractive salary packages with performance-based bonuses and a best-in-class employee share program. We know there are many aspects of our employees' lives that are important, and work is only one of these, so we offer benefits to enable your work to fit with your life. These benefits include flexible working options, a generous paid parental leave policy, other extended leave entitlements and parent rooms.
  
  
At BHP, we know that we are strengthened by diversity. We are an Equal Opportunity employer that is committed to making BHP a safe and inclusive workplace where everyone can thrive and be at their best every day. We are focused on creating a workforce that’s more diverse and represents the communities where we work and live. providing a work environment in which everyone is included, treated fairly and with respect. We are an Equal Opportunity employer and recognise that true diversity includes gender, age, race, disability status, sexual orientation, religion, neurodiversity, education levels, and many more aspects of your identity.
  
  
BHP is committed to providing a recruitment process that is fair, equitable and accessible for all. If you have a disability, we know that it may be helpful for us to adjust our process to make it equitable for your individual situation. If you would like to reach out to someone about your situation and our recruitment process, please email us at
***inclusion@bhp.com***
.

data.job_title: Coordinador(a) Mantenimiento Mina Equipos Móviles | Spence
data.description:
**Sobre Spence | BHP**
Spence es una operación minera ubicada en la Región de Antofagasta, reconocida por su liderazgo en innovación y sostenibilidad. Como parte de una empresa global dedicada a los recursos naturales, con presencia en más de 90 ubicaciones alrededor del mundo, impulsamos un modelo de minería autónoma que integra tecnología avanzada, eficiencia y seguridad, marcando el camino hacia el futuro.
  
  
Nuestro compromiso es generar valor de manera responsable, cuidando el medioambiente y poniendo a las personas en el centro de todo lo que hacemos.

En Spence creemos que el talento es la fuerza que transforma la minería. Aquí encontrarás oportunidades para crecer en un entorno inclusivo, diverso y orientado al aprendizaje continuo. Ser parte de Spence significa contribuir a proyectos que hacen historia, trabajando con equipos que comparten un propósito claro: innovar para construir un mundo más seguro, sostenible y humano. Tu voz cuenta, tu desarrollo importa y tu trabajo genera impacto real.
  
  
**El desafío que te espera**
Coordinador(a) de Mantenimiento Mina Equipos móviles deberá coordinar y controlar las tareas relacionadas con la preparación y ejecución de Mantenimiento de equipos móviles mina, de forma de asegurar un trabajo de calidad, en el tiempo comprometido y con los riesgos controlados.
  
  
**Principales responsabilidades**

- Cumplir con los programas anuales de seguridad del área.
- Gestionar la operatividad diaria requerida del turno (HSE, Plan Mtto, equipos)
- Asegurar el cumplimiento de las actividades de los supervisores de primera línea, asegurando Calidad y seguridad en la ejecución del plan de mtto. equipos móviles.
- Controlar y reportar el cumplimiento de KPI del área
- Controlar y reportar la disciplina de procesos de mantenimiento WM.
- Participar en reuniones de coordinación de trabajos con el área de operaciones mina, CAS, de forma de administrar de buena forma las interferencias y aprovechar las sinergias.
- Mejorar las prácticas del Mantenimiento de Equipos Móviles, haciendo el seguimiento de las desviaciones durante el desarrollo de la actividad.
- Definir e incorporar mejoras que permitan impactar positivamente en la seguridad, productividad y control de las tareas.
- Realizar coaching a la Supervisión de línea e ingenieros de servicio.
- Operar contratos del área.
- Validar modificaciones a Procedimientos de Trabajo y matrices de riesgo.

**Lo que esperamos de ti**

- Profesional titulado(a) Ingeniería Civil Mecánica/Eléctrico, carrera afín.
- Experiencia laboral comprobable de al menos 10 años en Mantenimiento equipos móviles.
- Conocimiento en SAP y/o FIORI para gestión de trabajos.
- Experiencia en mantención de equipos móviles CAT (CAEX, Cargadores, equipos de apoyo) y Komatsu (CAEX 980 y CGD WE-2350).
- Licencia clase B con experiencia en conducción interior mina.
- Disponibilidad para trabajar en faena por sistema de turnos 7x7.

**Por qué te encantará trabajar aquí**
En Spence | BHP entendemos que para dar lo mejor, necesitas sentirte bien. Por eso, nuestros beneficios están diseñados para acompañarte en cada etapa, cuidando tu salud, impulsando tu desarrollo y promoviendo un equilibrio real entre tu vida personal y laboral.
  
  

- Desarrollo profesional: Formación continua y oportunidades reales de crecimiento.
- Remuneración: justa y competitiva que refleje tu rol, habilidades y logros.
- Cultura inclusiva: Promovemos un entorno laboral donde cada persona se siente valorada, respetada y libre de expresar su identidad auténtica.
- Reconocimiento: Te recompensamos por tus pequeños y grandes logros a través de nuestro programa global de reconocimiento Big Thanks | Muchas Gracias.

¡Y mucho más!
  
  
**Sobre nuestro proceso**

*Si ya trabajas en BHP, postula a través del portal interno con tu correo corporativo.*
**¿Y por qué no tú?**
Con operaciones en todo el mundo, nuestra compañía te ofrece oportunidades sin límites. Desde beneficios competitivos hasta la posibilidad de trabajar en distintos países, aquí puedes diseñar tu carrera a tu manera. Si estás buscando un lugar donde tu trabajo tenga propósito y tu crecimiento sea parte del camino, este puede ser tu próximo paso.

data.job_title: Red de Talentos : Supervisor(a) Mantenimiento Mecánico Planta Concentradora
data.description:
**Acerca de BHP**
¡Te damos la bienvenida a BHP! Aquí impulsamos el desarrollo profesional de todos nuestros equipos para poder alcanzar su máximo potencial.
  
  
Con una cartera global de operaciones, ofrecemos un entorno diverso e inclusivo con extraordinarias oportunidades laborales. Nuestra estrategia es enfocarnos en crear un ambiente de trabajo seguro donde nuestros empleados se sientan fuertemente conectados con nuestra carta de valores, y donde sus competencias sean la clave para nuestro éxito.
  
  
¿Y por qué no tú? ¿Y por qué no BHP?
  
  
*Si ya estás empleado directamente por BHP, por favor inicia sesión con tu dirección de correo electrónico de BHP o postula a través de nuestro portal de empleos interno.*
**Acerca del Rol**
En BHP estamos permanentemente en búsqueda de las(os) mejores talentos. Si tienes experiencia en el área de mantenimiento mina y quieres sumarte a nuestro Grupo de Talentos para ser considerada(o) en procesos futuros, postula aquí!
  
  
Las futuras vacantes disponibles pueden ser en cualquiera de nuestras Faenas Mineras.
  
  
**Supervisor(a) Mantenimiento Mecánico Planta Concentradora**
Quién será responsable de asegurar la integridad tanto del personal que ejecuta las actividades de mantenimiento como de los equipos e instalaciones, supervisar y controlar el proceso de mantención en sus actividades y recursos de acuerdo a la estrategia de planificación, cumplir con los estándares HSE, desarrollar y mejorar procedimientos de trabajo y asegurar el cumplimiento de metas de disponibilidad y confiabilidad de equipos.
  
  
**Principales Responsabilidades**

- Asegurar la integridad de las personas y de los equipos en los procesos de mantención
- Supervisar el proceso de mantención mecánica, de los equipos de la concentradora de acuerdo con las normas y procedimientos establecidos, con el fin de asegurar el cumplimiento de la estrategia de mantención.
- Controlar y asegurar el cumplimiento de las métricas de control “Work Management” definidas para la Gerencia de Mantenimiento
- Verificar y asegurar la disponibilidad de equipos operativos y Stand By.
- Gestionar la documentación técnica, cambios de procesos y estándares
- Coordinar la recepción y entrega de equipos.
- Gestionar la implementación de planes del área de acuerdo a objetivos estratégicos, estándares de calidad, seguridad y medioambiente.
- Administrar uso de recursos para ejecutar los planes del área, optimizando los bienes e insumos disponibles.
- Gestionar el desarrollo de competencias y desempeño de las personas del área.
- Diseñar y gestionar acciones de mejoramiento para el desempeño de las operaciones del área.
- Demostrar e influir en el cumplimiento de los estándares HSE, la Carta de BHP y Código de Conducta.
- Liderar plan preparativos y actividades asociados al Shutdown
- Administrar uso de recursos para ejecutar los planes del área, optimizando los bienes e insumos disponibles.

**Acerca de Ti**

- Profesional titulado(a) en Ingeniería Mecánica/ Civil Industrial.
- Experiencia en posiciones de similar responsabilidad, como supervisor(a) en el área mecánica.
- Licencia de conducir Clase B (con dos años antigüedad)
- Experiencia de a lo menos 3 años en Mantenimiento en Planta Concentradora.
- Conocimiento en Monitoreo de Condiciones y mantenimiento centrado en confiabilidad.
- Conocimiento en SAP; Aprobación de Avisos, Cierre de OT.

**Requisitos excluyentes**

- Profesional titulado(a) en Ingeniería Mecánica/ Civil Industrial.
- Experiencia en posiciones de similar responsabilidad, como supervisor(a).
- Licencia de conducir Clase B (con dos años antigüedad)
- Experiencia de a lo menos 3 años en el Mantenimiento en Planta Concentradora

Apoyamos una Fuerza Laboral Diversa
  
  
En BHP nos adherimos a la Ley de Inclusión 21.015.
  
  
Sabemos que la diversidad nos fortalece. Es por eso por lo que estamos enfocados en crear una fuerza laboral más diversa que represente las comunidades en las que operamos y vivimos, asegurando un ambiente laboral donde cada persona sea incluida y tratada con respeto.
  
  
En ese sentido, estamos comprometidos a realizar un proceso de selección justo, equitativo y accesible para todos. Si tienes algún tipo de discapacidad, por favor escríbenos a inclusion@bhp.com para poder ajustar nuestro proceso de selección y brindarte el apoyo necesario.

data.job_title: Leadership Draglines & Dozers Opportunities Bowen Basin Talent Pool
data.description:
**About BHP**
At BHP we support our people to grow, learn, develop their skills and reach their potential. With a global portfolio of operations, we offer a diverse and inclusive environment with extraordinary career opportunities. Our strategy is to focus on creating a safe work environment where our employees feel strongly connected to our values and objectives, and where the capability of our people is key to our success.
  
  
Come and be a part of this success here
  
  
**About The Role**
We are building a
**talent pool of experienced and values-driven Superintendents Draglines & Dozers**
to support future opportunities across our operations. This is a great opportunity to step into a leadership role, leading high-performing teams and contributing to safe, efficient, and consistent operational outcomes.
  
  
Reporting to the Production leadership team, you will provide strong operational oversight of dragline and dozer activities. You will champion safety, support strategic and operational planning, drive performance, maintain cost discipline, and enable your team to perform at their best. Your ability to build relationships, apply systems thinking, and lead with discipline will be key to your success.
  
  
**Key Responsibilities**

- Lead and develop frontline and operational teams to deliver safe and productive mining performance
- Manage, coordinate, and verify operational activities across dragline operations, drill bench preparation, dozer push, and enabling mining tasks
- Maintain strong operational discipline with a focus on risk management and critical controls
- Engage teams in problem-solving, waste reduction, and continuous improvement initiatives
- Drive quality outcomes by setting clear expectations, verifying execution, and addressing issues early
- Foster a culture of accountability, collaboration, and continuous improvement
- Partner with planning, engineering, maintenance, and production teams to optimise fleet performance
- Contribute to medium- and long-term planning, including forecasting, improvement initiatives, and equipment strategy
- Support effective cost control through budget management, forecasting, and disciplined resource allocation
- Deliver operational and cost improvement initiatives aligned with business priorities

This opportunity is part of our
**talent community**
, where you will be considered for upcoming roles as they arise. It’s a great way to stay connected and be well-positioned when future vacancies become available.
  
  
**About You**

- Significant leadership experience in dragline or dozer operations (or related large scale mining operations).
- A strong commitment to safety and operational discipline.
- A demonstrated ability to build trusting relationships across teams and disciplines.
- A passion for continuous improvement, systems thinking, and structured problem solving. Exposure to LEAN management or Shingo principles will be highly valued.
- An ability to use systems thinking to understand how processes, behaviours, and operational factors interact to influence outcomes.
- Confidence in setting expectations, holding yourself and others accountable, and developing capability within your team

**About Our Process**
At BHP, we are committed to employing individuals who align with the BHP Charter Values and meet the requirements of the role. As part of the recruitment process, there are a number of checks which may be conducted to demonstrate applicant’s suitability for a role including police / criminal background checks, medical, drug and alcohol testing, due diligence checks, right to work checks, and/or reference checks.
  
  
**Supporting a Diverse Workforce**
The size, stability and magnitude of our business not only provides significant opportunity for professional development, but also attractive salary packages with performance-based bonuses and a best-in-class employee share program. We know there are many aspects of our employees' lives that are important, and work is only one of these, so we offer benefits to enable your work to fit with your life. These benefits include flexible working options, a generous paid parental leave policy, other extended leave entitlements and parent rooms.
  
  
At BHP, we know that we are strengthened by diversity. We are an Equal Opportunity employer that is committed to making BHP a safe and inclusive workplace where everyone can thrive and be at their best every day. We are focused on creating a workforce that’s more diverse and represents the communities where we work and live. providing a work environment in which everyone is included, treated fairly and with respect. We are an Equal Opportunity employer and recognise that true diversity includes gender, age, race, disability status, sexual orientation, religion, neurodiversity, education levels, and many more aspects of your identity.
  
  
BHP is committed to providing a recruitment process that is fair, equitable and accessible for all. If you have a disability, we know that it may be helpful for us to adjust our process to make it equitable for your individual situation. If you would like to reach out to someone about your situation and our recruitment process, please email us at inclusion@bhp.com.

data.description:
**About BHP**

At BHP we support our people to grow, learn, develop their skills and reach their potential.

With a global portfolio of operations, we offer a diverse and inclusive environment with extraordinary career opportunities. Our strategy is to focus on creating a safe work environment where our employees feel strongly connected to our values and objectives, and where the capability of our people is key to our success.

Come and be a part of this success.

  
  

**About the Role**

The Principal Research Engineer supports the acceleration of BHP's digital innovation and technology agenda. This role is foundational to Digital R&D, focusing on the discovery, validation and maturation of emerging technologies, shaping Digital R&D architecture, and building strong internal and external partnerships.

The role will establish and mature processes for innovation and technology acceleration across multiple Digital domains. It will enable delivery teams to move efficiently through critical decision points, improving cycle time and the quality of technology investment decisions. It will complement delivery teams by focusing on early-stage validation, technology maturity and decision support rather than end-to-end product delivery.

You will explore, stress test and integrate a combination of current, emerging and experimental technologies across existing enterprise capabilities (vendor, open-source and in-house).

Success in this role requires maintaining focus on rapid validation, evidence-based decision making, and clear recommendations for adoption or non-adoption in a complex stakeholder environment.

Key accountabilities include:

- Provide technical leadership across Digital R&D and contribute to standards and best practices
- Rapidly prototype and evaluate emerging technologies (incl. AI/ML) to generate evidence
- Lead integration of validated technologies into enterprise environments
- Translate ambiguous problem spaces into structured experiments and measurable outcomes
- Drive Proof of Concept and Proof of Value initiatives to validate, challenge and de-risk technology choices
- Identify and remove blockers across complex, cross-domain initiatives
- Improve processes, governance and ways of working to support scalable innovation
- Build strong relationships with stakeholders, vendors and research partners
- Mentor and guide teams without formal authority
- Enable enterprise-wide digital transformation through faster, evidence-based technology decisions

**About you**

You are:

- A strategic thinker who thrives in technically complex and evolving environments
- Comfortable navigating ambiguity and making progress with incomplete information
- An effective collaborator and influencer across diverse stakeholder groups
- Passionate about emerging technologies and their practical application at scale
- Focused on delivering measurable value through experimentation and iteration
- A supportive mentor who uplifts others through coaching and knowledge sharing

You have:

- Extensive experience (7+ years) in technology, Software or AI R&D or digital innovation in complex environments
- Demonstrated track record of progressing technologies from concept through to proof of value and production
- Strong capability in software engineering, rapid prototyping and/or solution architecture, including cloud-native, and API-driven environments
- Experience working across multiple technical domains and the full software development lifecycle
- Proven ability to engage and influence both technical and non-technical stakeholders

**About Our Process**

At BHP, we are committed to employing individuals who align with the BHP Charter Values and meet the requirements of the role. As part of the recruitment process, there are a number of checks which may be conducted to demonstrate applicants suitability for a role including police / criminal background checks, medical, drug and alcohol testing, due diligence checks, right to work checks, and/or reference checks.

If you are already employed directly by BHP, please log in using your BHP email address or apply via our internal jobs portal.

**Supporting a diverse workforce**

The size, stability and magnitude of our business not only provides significant opportunity for professional development, but also attractive salary packages with performance-based bonuses and a best-in-class employee share program. We know there are many aspects of our employees' lives that are important, and work is only one of these, so we offer benefits to enable your work to fit with your life. These benefits include flexible working options, a generous paid parental leave policy, other extended leave entitlements and parent rooms.

At BHP, we know that we are strengthened by diversity. We are an Equal Opportunity employer that is committed to making BHP a safe and inclusive workplace where everyone can thrive and be at their best every day. We are focused on creating a workforce that's more diverse and represents the communities where we work and live. providing a work environment in which everyone is included, treated fairly and with respect. We are an Equal Opportunity employer and recognise that true diversity includes gender, age, race, disability status, sexual orientation, religion, neurodiversity, education levels, and many more aspects of your identity.

BHP is committed to providing a recruitment process that is fair, equitable and accessible for all. If you have a disability, we know that it may be helpful for us to adjust our process to make it equitable for your individual situation. If you would like to reach out to someone about your situation and our recruitment process, please email us at [inclusion@bhp.com](mailto:inclusion@bhp.com).

company_object.name: BHP
company_object.long_description:
We are supplying the resources the world needs to help build a better, clearer future. Copper for renewable energy. Potash for sustainable farming. Iron ore and metallurgical coal for the steel needed for global infrastructure and the energy transition. #FutureIsClear

Across our global operations, we are committed to working in ways that are true to our BHP Charter values of Sustainability, Integrity, Respect, Performance, Simplicity and Accountability. Learn more about working at BHP and the exciting career opportunities that exist for professionals, undergraduates and graduates on our website: www.bhp.com.

data.description:
**Sobre Spence | BHP**
Spence es una operación minera ubicada en la Región de Antofagasta, reconocida por su liderazgo en innovación y sostenibilidad. Como parte de una empresa global dedicada a los recursos naturales, con presencia en más de 90 ubicaciones alrededor del mundo, impulsamos un modelo de minería autónoma que integra tecnología avanzada, eficiencia y seguridad, marcando el camino hacia el futuro.
  
  
Nuestro compromiso es generar valor de manera responsable, cuidando el medioambiente y poniendo a las personas en el centro de todo lo que hacemos.
  
  
**¿Por qué Spence | BHP?**
En Spence creemos que el talento es la fuerza que transforma la minería. Aquí encontrarás oportunidades para crecer en un entorno inclusivo, diverso y orientado al aprendizaje continuo. Ser parte de Spence significa contribuir a proyectos que hacen historia, trabajando con equipos que comparten un propósito claro: innovar para construir un mundo más seguro, sostenible y humano. Tu voz cuenta, tu desarrollo importa y tu trabajo genera impacto real.
  
  
**El desafío que te espera**
Tu misión principal será asegurar la estabilidad, eficiencia y optimización de los procesos de producción de cobre y molibdeno dentro de la cadena de valor de la operación de la planta concentradora. Tendrás la responsabilidad de impulsar la productividad y la mejora continua en las áreas de flotación colectiva, flotación selectiva, concentraducto, y filtrado de cobre y molibdeno, resguardando operaciones seguras, confiables y sustentables.
  
  
Trabajarás de manera colaborativa con equipos de Metalurgia, Mantenimiento, CAS, Ingeniería y empresas colaboradoras, contribuyendo al cumplimiento de metas de producción y disponibilidad. Desde tu rol, detectarás desviaciones críticas, implementarás soluciones robustas y promoverás la estandarización técnica en la gestión de la producción.
  
  
**Principales Responsabilidades**

- Cumplir y promover los lineamientos del Field Leadership Program.
- Asegurar el cumplimiento de normas de seguridad, medio ambiente y salud ocupacional.
- Supervisar la operación y desempeño de las áreas operativas de la superintendencia de concentración y filtrado de cobre-molibdeno.
- Mantener la estabilidad operativa y proponer ajustes para maximizar recuperación de cobre y molibdeno.
- Identificar desviaciones y liderar análisis de causa raíz.
- Liderar proyectos de mejoramiento en la superintendencia.
- Elaborar reportes de desempeño de control y KPIs.
- Coordinar acciones entre Metalurgia, Mantenimiento, Ingeniería, CAS y Proyectos.
- Brindar soporte técnico especializado a equipos operativos y de ingeniería.
- Participar en comisionamientos, revisiones de ingeniería y puesta en marcha.
- Capacitar a personal operativo en estrategias de operación.

**Lo que esperamos de ti**

- Titulación en Ingeniería Civil Metalúrgica, Química o carrera similar.
- Experiencia profesional mínima de 7 a 10 años, con al menos 5 años en operación de plantas concentradoras.
- Experiencia en procesos de flotación de cobre y molibdeno, remolienda y plantas de filtrado de cobre.
- Habilidades para liderar equipos multidisciplinarios.
- Disponibilidad para trabajar en faena en sistema de turno 7x7 día.

**Por qué te encantará trabajar aquí**
En Spence | BHP entendemos que para dar lo mejor, necesitas sentirte bien. Por eso, nuestros beneficios están diseñados para acompañarte en cada etapa, cuidando tu salud, impulsando tu desarrollo y promoviendo un equilibrio real entre tu vida personal y laboral.
  
  

- Desarrollo profesional: Formación continua y oportunidades reales de crecimiento.
- Remuneración: justa y competitiva que refleje tu rol, habilidades y logros.
- Cultura inclusiva: Promovemos un entorno laboral donde cada persona se siente valorada, respetada y libre de expresar su identidad auténtica.
- Reconocimiento: Te recompensamos por tus pequeños y grandes logros a través de nuestro programa global de reconocimiento Big Thanks | Muchas Gracias.

¡Y mucho más!
  
  
**Sobre nuestro proceso**
En Spence | BHP contratamos personas que se alinean con nuestros valores y cumplen con los requisitos del rol. Como parte del proceso, podríamos someter a revisión el cumplimiento efectivo de los requisitos informados para determinar la idoneidad y capacidad personal para ejecutar el trabajo.
  
  
*Si ya trabajas en BHP, postula a través del portal interno con tu correo corporativo.*
**¿Y por qué no tú?**
Con operaciones en todo el mundo, nuestra compañía te ofrece oportunidades sin límites. Desde beneficios competitivos hasta la posibilidad de trabajar en distintos países, aquí puedes diseñar tu carrera a tu manera. Si estás buscando un lugar donde tu trabajo tenga propósito y tu crecimiento sea parte del camino, este puede ser tu próximo paso.
  
  
¡Postula hoy y sé parte de la transformación de la minería!
  
  
**Apoyamos una fuerza laboral diversa**
En Spence | BHP nos adherimos a la Ley de Inclusión 21.015.
  
  
Sabemos que la diversidad nos fortalece. Es por eso por lo que estamos enfocados en crear una fuerza laboral más diversa que represente las comunidades en las que operamos y vivimos, asegurando un ambiente laboral donde cada persona sea incluida y tratada con respeto.
  
  
En ese sentido, estamos comprometidos a realizar un proceso de selección justo, equitativo y accesible para todos. Si tienes algún tipo de discapacidad, por favor escríbenos a inclusion@bhp.com para poder ajustar nuestro proceso de selección y brindarte el apoyo necesario.

data.job_title: Material Handling System Supervisor (MHS) (Underground) - Carrapateena - FIFO 1
data.description:
**About BHP**
At BHP we support our people to grow, learn, develop their skills and reach their potential. With a global portfolio of operations, we offer a diverse and inclusive environment with extraordinary career opportunities. Our strategy is to focus on creating a safe work environment where our employees feel strongly connected to our values and objectives, and where the capability of our people is key to our success.
  
  
Come and be a part of this success.
  
  
**Our Operations - Carrapateena**
Located 472 kilometres north-west of Adelaide, South Australia, Carrapateena is part of our Minerals Australia operations, within the Copper South Australia province. The mine is located on the traditional land of the Kokatha people. Carrapateena produces copper concentrate and is one of the largest mining projects in South Australia in the last decade.
  
  
**Please note this position is based at Carrapateena, operating on an 8/6, 7/7 day/night roster pattern arrangement with FIFO from Adelaide OR Port Augusta.**
**About The Role**
Reporting to the Underground Material Handling System (MHS) Supervisor, the Belt Technician is a key role in ensuring that the operational tasks associated with the MHS system are carried out safely and effectively to support Carrapateena in achieving their production targets. The MHS system consists of conveyors, transfer chutes and feeders which are located underground and on the surface. The role also focuses heavily on housekeeping standards in these areas and basic conveyor belt inspections and maintenance
  
  
In this role you will:-
  
  

- Integrate with Maintenance counterparts to ensure effective, pro-actively planned maintenance to minimise unplanned downtime
- Ensure daily operational tasks are completed and job history recorded in SAP.
- monitor conveyor belt operation, identifying issues and escalating to maintenance team in timely manner
- Maintain high housekeeping standards for all the equipment
- Assist where required in the permit to work system, including helping update documents relating to specific tasks.
- Provide continual feedback to the Shift Maintenance Supervisors on job progress

**About You**
Our successful candidate: --
  
  

- Background as a Conveyor Belt Technician or Conveyor Scraper Technician
- Strong knowledge of conveyor belt systems including belt tracking, skirting, and cleaners/scrapers
- Experience performing routine inspections and maintenance reporting
- Exposure to working at heights and confined spaces
- Proven experience working around conveyors systems.
- Proven commitment to look after your own wellbeing and the confidence to speak up when others put themselves in harm's way
- Hold extensive experience driving a manual vehicle.
- Self-motivated, driven and focused on achieving high performance targets whilst always prioritizing safety first
- I.T, Bobcat, riggers, dogging and crane ticket advantageous, but not essential
- Ability to communicate across multiple levels and disciplines
- Whilst not essential, experience working with SAP would be of advantage

**Diversity & Inclusion**
Carrapateena thrives on a diverse workforce, providing an energetic & inclusive team of individuals of varying backgrounds.
  
  
**Benefits**
BHP offers an array of attractive perks to our employees, not limited to annual bonus, shares program, employee recognition program, heavily discounted corporate offers and so much more. To hear more about the benefits we offer our employees, please take a moment to play Why choose BHP for your career? https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=W541c1oeLmE
  
  
**APPLICATIONS CLOSE: Monday 29 June 2026 at Midnight SA TIME**
**About Our Process**
At BHP, we are committed to employing individuals who align with the BHP Charter Values and meet the requirements of the role. As part of the recruitment process, there are a number of checks which may be conducted to demonstrate applicants’ suitability for a role including police / criminal background checks, medical, drug and alcohol testing, due diligence checks, right to work checks, and/or reference checks.
  
  
If you are already employed directly by BHP, please log in using your BHP email address or apply via our internal jobs portal.
  
  
**Supporting a Diverse Workforce**
The size, stability and magnitude of our business not only provides significant opportunity for professional development, but also attractive salary packages with performance-based bonuses and a best-in-class employee share program. We know there are many aspects of our employees' lives that are important, and work is only one of these, so we offer benefits to enable your work to fit with your life. These benefits include flexible working options, a generous paid parental leave policy, other extended leave entitlements and parent rooms.
  
  
At BHP, we know that we are strengthened by diversity. We are an Equal Opportunity employer that is committed to making BHP a safe and inclusive workplace where everyone can thrive and be at their best every day. We are focused on creating a workforce that’s more diverse and represents the communities where we work and live. providing a work environment in which everyone is included, treated fairly and with respect. We are an Equal Opportunity employer and recognise that true diversity includes gender, age, race, disability status, sexual orientation, religion, neurodiversity, education levels, and many more aspects of your identity.
  
  
BHP is committed to providing a recruitment process that is fair, equitable and accessible for all. If you have a disability, we know that it may be helpful for us to adjust our process to make it equitable for your individual situation. If you would like to reach out to someone about your situation and our recruitment process, please email us at
***inclusion@bhp.com***
.

data.job_title: Supervisor(a) Mantención Equipos Transversales | Minera Escondida
data.description:
**Sobre Escondida | BHP**
En Chile, nuestra operación tiene una identidad única que refleja nuestro compromiso de crear un futuro más sostenible. Escondida, ubicada en el desierto de Atacama, es la mayor productora de cobre del mundo y un motor clave para la transición hacia energías limpias. Operamos con energía 100 % renovable y agua desalinizada, liderando la minería responsable e innovadora que conecta Chile con el futuro global.
  
  
**¿Por qué Escondida | BHP?**
Porque en Escondida, operada por BHP, creemos que nuestro propósito va mucho más allá de la minería: trabajamos para mejorar vidas y construir un futuro mejor para todos. Somos operados por una compañía global líder en recursos naturales, con operaciones en más de 90 ubicaciones alrededor del mundo.
  
  
Ser parte de nuestros equipos significa unirte a una empresa que cree en el poder de las ideas valientes, en la fuerza de los equipos diversos y en el impacto positivo que cada persona puede generar. Aquí, tu voz cuenta. Tu crecimiento importa. Y tu trabajo contribuye directamente a transformar el mundo.
  
  
**Acerca del Rol**
En Minera Escondida, estamos en busca de nuestro próximo(a) Supervisor(a) Mantención Equipos Transversales, para asegurar la correcta ejecución de los trabajos de mantención en los puentes grúas, aires acondicionados y redes contra incendio de la compañía, velar por la salud y seguridad de las personas y calidad en los trabajos realizados garantizando de esta forma la continuidad operacional y calidad de servicio a los diferentes clientes internos.
  
  
**Principales Responsabilidades**

- Asegurar el cumplimiento de los estándares, procedimientos y normativos en los trabajos que se realizan en terreno
- Analizar y gestionar los riesgos de las actividades de su responsabilidad
- Administrar y gestionar de manera eficiente los recursos
- Priorizar las actividades, tanto del programa como de los trabajos imprevistos, en función del riesgo y criticidad de cada equipo.
- Gestionar con los clientes la recepción y entrega de equipos, ya sea para mantención programada y/o imprevisto.
- Identificar oportunidades de mejora en ámbitos HSE y operacional en la ejecución de la mantención
- Participar activamente en el proceso de planificación de los recursos
- Evaluar y aprobar requerimientos de trabajos identificados dentro del área de responsabilidad
- Responsable por el cumplimiento de todos los KPI´s de WM asociados a la ejecución de actividades
- Monitorear e identificar competencias del personal a su cargo, tanto, técnicas como seguridad
- Promover y liderar la reportabilidad de eventos de seguridad, tanto en tiempo como en forma
- Generar reportes e informes operacionales como de falla, según corresponda
- Liderar procesos de eliminación de defecto y mejora continua

**Acerca de Ti**

- Ingeniero(a) Civil o Ejecución eléctrico (requisito excluyente)
- Licencia SEC clase A (requisito excluyente)
- Experiencia de 2 años como supervisor de mantención (requisito excluyente)
- Licencia de conducir clase B, al menos 2 años de antigüedad (requisito excluyente)
- 5 años en actividades ligadas a mantención
- Experiencia liderando equipos
- Requisitos Deseable:
- Conocimiento normativo en riesgo eléctrico (NFPA 70 E)
- Conocimiento en normativa aplicable a equipos de izaje
- Experiencia en SAP, modulo mantención
- Experiencia en procesos de eliminación de fallas
- Ingles nivel intermedio (hablado y escrito)

**Por qué te encantará trabajar aquí**
En Escondida | BHP entendemos que para dar lo mejor, necesitas sentirte bien. Por eso, nuestros beneficios están diseñados para acompañarte en cada etapa, cuidando tu salud, impulsando tu desarrollo y promoviendo un equilibrio real entre tu vida personal y laboral.
  
  

- Desarrollo profesional: Formación continua y oportunidades reales de crecimiento.
- Remuneración: justa y competitiva que refleje tu rol, habilidades y logros.
- Cultura inclusiva: Promovemos un entorno laboral donde cada persona se siente valorada, respetada y libre de expresar su identidad auténtica.
- Reconocimiento: Te recompensamos por tus pequeños y grandes logros a través de nuestro programa global de reconocimiento Big Thanks | Muchas Gracias.

¡Y mucho más!
  
  
**Sobre nuestro proceso**
En Escondida | BHP contratamos personas que se alinean con nuestros valores y cumplen con los requisitos del rol. Como parte del proceso, podríamos analizar el cumplimiento de los requisitos para determinar la idoneidad y capacidad personal para ejecutar el trabajo.
  
  
*Si ya trabajas en BHP, postula a través del portal interno con tu correo corporativo.*
**¿Y por qué no tú?**
Nuestra compañía te ofrece oportunidades sin límites. Desde beneficios competitivos hasta la posibilidad de trabajar en distintos países, aquí puedes diseñar tu carrera a tu manera. Si estás buscando un lugar donde tu trabajo tenga propósito y tu crecimiento sea parte del camino, este puede ser tu próximo paso.
  
  
¡Postula hoy y sé parte de la transformación de la minería!
  
  
**Apoyamos una fuerza laboral diversa**
En Escondida | BHP nos adherimos a la Ley de Inclusión 21.015.
  
  
Sabemos que la diversidad nos fortalece. Es por eso por lo que estamos enfocados en crear una fuerza laboral más diversa que represente las comunidades en las que operamos y vivimos, asegurando un ambiente laboral donde cada persona sea incluida y tratada con respeto.
  
  
En ese sentido, estamos comprometidos a realizar un proceso de selección justo, equitativo y accesible para todos. Si tienes algún tipo de discapacidad, por favor escríbenos a inclusion@bhp.com para poder ajustar nuestro proceso de selección y brindarte el apoyo necesario.

data.description:
**About BHP**

At BHP we support our people to grow, learn, develop their skills and reach their potential. With a global portfolio of operations, we offer a diverse and inclusive environment with extraordinary career opportunities.

Our strategy is to focus on creating a safe work environment where our employees feel strongly connected to our values and objectives and where the capability of our people is key to our success.

Come and be a part of this success.  
  
This opportunity is currently open to external applicants only. Internal opportunities will be shared separately.

  
  

**About Copper South Australia**

BHP's Copper South Australia (Copper SA) asset comprises three world-class operations, Olympic Dam, Carrapateena and Prominent Hill, supported by a central smelter and refinery, alongside the Oak Dam exploration project. Together, these assets host some of the world's most significant deposits of copper, gold, silver and uranium. With a strong pipeline of growth projects across the portfolio, there's never been a more exciting time to join BHP in South Australia.  
  
**About the Role**

We are seeking motivated and passionate applicants to join our 2027 Apprenticeship Program, with opportunities available across Olympic Dam, Carrapateena and Prominent Hill, commencing in January 2027.  
  
Over the course of this four-year apprenticeship, you'll gain hands-on, industry-leading training and mentoring from BHP's experienced workforce. You can expect excellent working conditions, enrolment with a Registered Training Organisation (RTO) and the opportunity to achieve a nationally recognised qualification in your trade upon successful completion.  
  
At BHP, we are committed to fostering an inclusive and diverse workplace that reflects the communities in which we operate. We value high performance, safety, teamwork, strong Indigenous participation and gender balance, creating an environment where everyone can thrive.

Nationally Accredited Trades Certificates being offered may include:

• Auto Electrical

• Boilermaker

• Electrical

• Mobile Plant (Heavy Diesel)

• Mechanical Fitting

**What can BHP offer you?**

• Hands on training with an industry leader

• Nationally accredited qualifications

• Skills & experience to gain a career as a trades person in the resources industry

• Work as part of a collaborative & supportive environment

• Competitive remuneration package and employee benefits

• First-rate working conditions and development opportunities

If successful, apprentices will be employed under a 4-year fixed term contract. Apprentices are expected to work and attend Trade School in South Australia as per South Australian Apprenticeship requirements.

**WHY CHOOSE BHP FOR YOUR CAREER?**

**About You**

These opportunities are available for enthusiastic and motivated individuals wanting to obtain a trade and embark on a career within the mining sector!

We are eager to engage with people who have a genuine interest in their nominated trade. We are looking for adaptable, positive people that are open to exploring the world of mining while learning something they have a demonstrated passion for.

The ideal candidate we are seeking will:

• Demonstrate interest in your nominated trade through work experience and/or hobbies

• Hold an overriding commitment to health, safety, environmental responsibility, and sustainable development

• Have a strong desire to learn and develop a range of new skills with the ability to combine work and study commitments

• Be motivated to achieve individual and team goals and objectives, with a willingness to actively contribute to outcomes

**About Our Process**

At BHP, we are committed to employing individuals who align with the BHP Charter Values and meet the requirements of the role. As part of the recruitment process, there are a number of checks which may be conducted to demonstrate applicants' suitability for a role including police / criminal background checks, medical, drug and alcohol testing, due diligence checks, right to work checks, and/or reference checks.  
  
**Supporting a Diverse Workforce**

The size, stability and magnitude of our business not only provides significant opportunity for professional development, but also attractive salary packages with performance-based bonuses and a best-in-class employee share program. We know there are many aspects of our employees' lives that are important and work is only one of these, so we offer benefits to enable your work to fit with your life. These benefits include flexible working options, a generous paid parental leave policy, other extended leave entitlements and parent rooms.   
  
At BHP, we know that we are strengthened by diversity. We are an equal opportunity employer that is committed to making BHP a safe and inclusive workplace where everyone can thrive and be at their best every day. We are focused on creating a workforce that's more diverse and represents the communities where we work and live, providing a work environment in which everyone is included, treated fairly and with respect. We recognise that true diversity includes gender, age, race, disability status, sexual orientation, religion, neurodiversity, education levels and many more aspects of your identity.  
  
BHP is committed to providing a recruitment process that is fair, equitable and accessible for all. If you have a disability, we know that it may be helpful for us to adjust our process to make it equitable for your individual situation. If you would like to reach out to someone about your situation and our recruitment process, please email us at **[inclusion@bhp.com](mailto:inclusion@bhp.com)**

data.job_title: Principal Marketing Strategy & Planning | Singapore| Saskatoon
data.description:
**About BHP**
At BHP we support our people to grow, learn, develop their skills and reach their potential. With a global portfolio of operations, we offer a diverse and inclusive environment with extraordinary career opportunities. Our strategy is to focus on creating a safe work environment where our employees feel strongly connected to our values and objectives, and where the capability of our people is key to our success.
  
  
Come and be a part of this success.
  
  
**About The Role**
Reporting to the Head of Marketing Strategy, you will lead the establishment and maintenance of Potash placement and pricing strategy—grounded in a current view of market dynamics and developed in close engagement with stakeholders across Sales & Marketing, the Asset, MAE and Leadership.
  
  
**Key Accountabilities**

- Develop and maintain the placement strategy, defining where and how we sell to strengthen market access and value realisation.
- Develop and review contract pricing strategy, balancing commercial & operational objectives
- Establish and maintain commercial benchmarks to support consistent, evidence‑based decision‑making.
- Maintain an up‑to‑date view of the Potash market (supply/demand, pricing, trade flows, competitor and customer dynamics) and translate insights into implications for strategy and execution.
- Support the translation of strategic direction into our Placement Plan.
- Lead Potash price forecasting from a Marketing perspective, consolidating Marketing views on market drivers, assumptions and scenarios (informed by an ongoing Potash market view) that feed into the MAE price forecast.
- Own and periodically refresh the long‑term Marketing strategy, including product strategy, placement strategy and position in the value chain.
- Provide Marketing input to Potash Asset strategy and related studies, bringing market context and commercial insight to key decisions.

**About You**

- Demonstrated strategic thinking, including the ability to synthesise inputs, structure problems and turn ambiguity into clear options and recommendations.
- Deep commercial and analytical capability, including experience developing market views, benchmarks and scenarios.
- Ability to analyse commodity markets, interpret market signals and develop a clear view of key drivers, risks and opportunities.
- Proven stakeholder engagement and influence—able to co-create strategy across multiple teams and senior leaders.
- Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to tailor messages from detailed analysis to executive-level storytelling.
- Experience leading strategy development in Potash or another relevant commodity (highly regarded).
- Bachelor’s degree in a commercial, economic, technical and/or relevant business discipline.

If you are interested in a role that combines strategic thinking with cross‑functional influence in a dynamic market, please submit your application.
  
  
This role can be located in either Singapore or Saskatoon
  
  
**About Our Process**
At BHP, we are committed to employing individuals who align with the BHP Charter Values and meet the requirements of the role. As part of the recruitment process, there are a number of checks which may be conducted to demonstrate applicants suitability for a role including police / criminal background checks, medical, drug and alcohol testing, due diligence checks, right to work checks, and/or reference checks.
  
  
*If you are already employed directly by BHP, please log in using your BHP email address or apply via our internal jobs portal.*
**Supporting a Diverse Workforce**
The size, stability and magnitude of our business not only provides significant opportunity for professional development, but also attractive salary packages with performance-based bonuses and a best-in-class employee share program. We know there are many aspects of our employees' lives that are important, and work is only one of these, so we offer benefits to enable your work to fit with your life. These benefits include flexible working options, a generous paid parental leave policy, other extended leave entitlements and parent rooms.
  
  
At BHP, we know that we are strengthened by diversity. We are an Equal Opportunity employer that is committed to making BHP a safe and inclusive workplace where everyone can thrive and be at their best every day. We are focused on creating a workforce that’s more diverse and represents the communities where we work and live. providing a work environment in which everyone is included, treated fairly and with respect. We are an Equal Opportunity employer and recognise that true diversity includes gender, age, race, disability status, sexual orientation, religion, neurodiversity, education levels, and many more aspects of your identity.
  
  
BHP is committed to providing a recruitment process that is fair, equitable and accessible for all. If you have a disability, we know that it may be helpful for us to adjust our process to make it equitable for your individual situation. If you would like to reach out to someone about your situation and our recruitment process, please email us at
***inclusion@bhp.com***
.

company_object.name: BHP
company_object.long_description:
We are supplying the resources the world needs to help build a better, clearer future. Copper for renewable energy. Potash for sustainable farming. Iron ore and metallurgical coal for the steel needed for global infrastructure and the energy transition. #FutureIsClear

Across our global operations, we are committed to working in ways that are true to our BHP Charter values of Sustainability, Integrity, Respect, Performance, Simplicity and Accountability. Learn more about working at BHP and the exciting career opportunities that exist for professionals, undergraduates and graduates on our website: www.bhp.com.
